title: NullPointerException in order processing service
input:
2026-04-14 09:23:17.445 ERROR [order-service,tid=http-nio-8080-exec-14] c.a.order.service.OrderService - Failed to process order for customer request
java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "com.acme.order.model.Address.getZipCode()" because the return value of "com.acme.order.model.Customer.getShippingAddress()" is null
	at com.acme.order.service.OrderService.calculateShipping(OrderService.java:87)
	at com.acme.order.service.OrderService.processOrder(OrderService.java:54)
	at com.acme.order.controller.OrderController.submitOrder(OrderController.java:41)
	at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.DirectMethodHandleAccessor.invoke(DirectMethodHandleAccessor.java:103)
	at org.springframework.web.servlet.FrameworkServlet.service(FrameworkServlet.java:885)
	at org.apache.catalina.core.ApplicationFilterChain.internalDoFilter(ApplicationFilterChain.java:178)
2026-04-14 09:23:17.446 WARN  [order-service,tid=http-nio-8080-exec-14] c.a.order.controller.OrderController - Returning 500 to client for orderId=ORD-88214
expectedObservations: Code attempted to call Address.getZipCode() on the null address
Error occurred on thread http-nio-8080-exec-14 in order-service
HTTP 500 returned to client for orderId=ORD-88214
expectedCauses: Customer record has no shipping address populated — missing validation before order submission
Upstream form or API allowed order creation without a required shipping address
expectedNextSteps: Add null check or validation for shippingAddress before calling calculateShipping
Query the database for customer linked to ORD-88214 to confirm whether shippingAddress is missing or was lost in transit
Review OrderService.java:87 and add defensive guard or @NotNull constraint on the Customer.shippingAddress field
Check if this is a regression by reviewing recent changes to the order submission flow

title: IllegalStateException from Kafka consumer rebalance during message processing
input:
2026-04-14 11:05:42.318 ERROR [payment-consumer,tid=kafka-consumer-payment-group-3] c.a.payment.consumer.PaymentEventConsumer - Error processing payment event from topic payments.completed
java.lang.IllegalStateException: This consumer has already been closed.
	at org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.KafkaConsumer.acquireAndEnsureOpen(KafkaConsumer.java:2762)
	at org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.KafkaConsumer.commitSync(KafkaConsumer.java:1510)
	at com.acme.payment.consumer.PaymentEventConsumer.handlePayment(PaymentEventConsumer.java:63)
	at com.acme.payment.consumer.PaymentEventConsumer.onMessage(PaymentEventConsumer.java:38)
	at org.springframework.kafka.listener.KafkaMessageListenerContainer$ListenerConsumer.invokeListener(KafkaMessageListenerContainer.java:2078)
2026-04-14 11:05:42.112 WARN  [payment-consumer,tid=kafka-consumer-payment-group-3] o.a.k.c.c.internals.ConsumerCoordinator - [Consumer clientId=consumer-payment-group-3, groupId=payment-group] Revoke previously assigned partitions payments.completed-0, payments.completed-1, payments.completed-2
2026-04-14 11:05:41.998 INFO  [payment-consumer,tid=kafka-consumer-payment-group-3] o.a.k.c.c.internals.ConsumerCoordinator - [Consumer clientId=consumer-payment-group-3, groupId=payment-group] Request joining group due to: group is already rebalancing
2026-04-14 11:05:33.005 WARN  [payment-consumer,tid=kafka-consumer-payment-group-3] o.a.k.c.c.internals.AbstractCoordinator - [Consumer clientId=consumer-payment-group-3, groupId=payment-group] consumer poll timeout has expired. This means the time between subsequent calls to poll() was longer than the configured max.poll.interval.ms, which typically implies that the poll loop is spending too much time processing messages. You can address this by increasing max.poll.interval.ms or by reducing the maximum size of batches returned in poll() with max.poll.records.
expectedObservations: IllegalStateException: consumer has already been closed, thrown at PaymentEventConsumer.handlePayment(PaymentEventConsumer.java:63)
Consumer attempted commitSync after the KafkaConsumer was closed
Consumer poll timeout expired — time between poll() calls exceeded max.poll.interval.ms
Consumer group payment-group triggered rebalance and revoked partitions payments.completed-0, -1, -2
Kafka warning explicitly states poll loop is spending too much time processing messages
expectedCauses: Message processing in handlePayment takes longer than max.poll.interval.ms, causing the broker to revoke partitions and close the consumer while processing is still in-flight
Batch size (max.poll.records) may be too large, causing cumulative processing time to exceed the poll interval
expectedNextSteps: Increase max.poll.interval.ms in consumer configuration to accommodate actual processing time
Reduce max.poll.records to limit the number of messages fetched per poll cycle
Profile PaymentEventConsumer.handlePayment to identify why processing is slow (e.g., blocking I/O, downstream calls)
Consider making the offset commit asynchronous or moving heavy processing to a separate thread pool

title: HikariCP connection pool timeout under sustained load
input:
2026-04-14 14:32:05.221 WARN  [inventory-service,tid=http-nio-8080-exec-7] com.zaxxer.hikari.pool.HikariPool - HikariPool-1 - Connection is not available, request timed out after 30000ms.
2026-04-14 14:32:05.223 ERROR [inventory-service,tid=http-nio-8080-exec-7] c.a.inventory.service.InventoryService - Failed to check stock for SKU=WIDGET-4490
org.springframework.jdbc.CannotGetJdbcConnectionException: Failed to obtain JDBC connection
	at org.springframework.jdbc.datasource.DataSourceUtils.getConnection(DataSourceUtils.java:84)
	at org.springframework.jdbc.core.JdbcTemplate.execute(JdbcTemplate.java:376)
	at com.acme.inventory.service.InventoryService.getStockLevel(InventoryService.java:44)
	at com.acme.inventory.controller.InventoryController.checkStock(InventoryController.java:28)
Caused by: java.sql.SQLTransientConnectionException: HikariPool-1 - Connection is not available, request timed out after 30000ms.
	at com.zaxxer.hikari.pool.HikariPool.createTimeoutException(HikariPool.java:686)
	at com.zaxxer.hikari.pool.HikariPool.getConnection(HikariPool.java:179)
2026-04-14 14:32:05.224 WARN  [inventory-service,tid=http-nio-8080-exec-7] com.zaxxer.hikari.pool.HikariPool - HikariPool-1 - Pool stats (total=10, active=10, idle=0, waiting=23)
2026-04-14 14:32:01.887 WARN  [inventory-service,tid=HikariPool-1 housekeeper] com.zaxxer.hikari.pool.HikariPool - HikariPool-1 - Thread starvation or clock leap detected (housekeeper delta=1m32s).
2026-04-14 14:31:58.102 WARN  [inventory-service,tid=http-nio-8080-exec-3] com.zaxxer.hikari.pool.HikariPool - HikariPool-1 - Connection is not available, request timed out after 30000ms.
2026-04-14 14:31:55.009 WARN  [inventory-service,tid=http-nio-8080-exec-11] com.zaxxer.hikari.pool.HikariPool - HikariPool-1 - Connection is not available, request timed out after 30000ms.
expectedObservations: HikariPool-1 connection request timed out after 30000ms — multiple occurrences across threads exec-3, exec-7, exec-11
Pool stats show total=10, active=10, idle=0, waiting=23 — pool fully exhausted with 23 requests queued
Thread starvation or clock leap detected with housekeeper delta=1m32s
Failure affected stock check for SKU=WIDGET-4490
expectedCauses: Connection pool size (10) is too small for current request concurrency — 23 threads are waiting for a connection
Long-running or leaked database connections are holding all 10 pool slots, preventing recycling
Thread starvation (housekeeper delta=1m32s) suggests the JVM is under severe CPU or GC pressure, delaying connection management
expectedNextSteps: Increase HikariCP maximumPoolSize in application configuration (e.g., from 10 to 20-30) based on expected concurrency
Investigate the thread starvation warning — check GC logs, CPU utilization, and thread dumps at the time of the incident
Review slow query logs on the database to identify queries that hold connections for extended periods
Add connection leak detection by setting spring.datasource.hikari.leak-detection-threshold to a value like 30000
Monitor the database server's max_connections to ensure it can handle the increased pool size
